reasoning_content: We need to determine if 359 is a prime number. A prime number is a natural number greater than 1 that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself. Check divisibility: 359 is not even, not divisible by 3 (sum digits 3+5+9=17, not divisible by 3), not divisible by 5 (ends with 9), check 7: 7*51=357, remainder 2, so not divisible. 11: 11*32=352, remainder 7, not. 13: 13*27=351, remainder 8, not. 17: 17*21=357, remainder 2, not. 19: 19*18=342, remainder 17, not. 23: 23*15=345, remainder 14, not. 29: 29*12=348, remainder 11, not. 31: 31*11=341, remainder 18, not. 37: 37*9=333, remainder 26, not. 41: 41*8=328, remainder 31, not. 43: 43*8=344, remainder 15, not. 47: 47*7=329, remainder 30, not. 53: 53*6=318, remainder 41, not. 59: 59*6=354, remainder 5, not. We only need to check up to sqrt(359) ~ 18.95, so up to 17. We already checked 2,3,5,7,11,13,17. None divide. So 359 is prime. Answer: yes.
